question_text: Two bar magnets are placed with their north poles facing each other. Describe what will happen and explain why.
correct_answer: The magnets will repel (push away from) each other because like poles repel each other.
explanation: When two magnets have the same poles facing each other (both north or both south), they repel. This is one of the fundamental rules of magnetism: like poles repel and opposite poles attract.
